title: Comforting Crunchy Tunisian Lamb Flatbread
description: A tangy and pillowy Tunisian dish featuring herbes-de-provence and long-fermentation-dough, perfect for a quick-fix meal, this anti-inflammatory-friendly flatbread is filled with lamb and spices.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Roll out the long-fermentation-dough to a thickness of about 1/4 inch.
Mix the ground lamb shoulder with herbes-de-provence, olive oil, onion, garlic, salt, black pepper, turmeric, and paprika in a bowl.
Spread the lamb mixture evenly over the dough, leaving a 1 inch border around the edges.
Fold the edges of the dough over the lamb mixture to form a crust.
Place the flatbread on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Bake the flatbread in the preheated oven for 25 minutes.
Remove the flatbread from the oven and let it cool for 5 minutes before slicing.
Serve the flatbread warm and enjoy.
